_readme: The GENERIC base layer — the foundational knob values every render inherits (resolution: CLI > recipe.json > THIS FILE). These are honest, measured STARTING POINTS, not a universal look: most were calibrated by single-knob ladders on ONE underexposed DSLR wide-field (the 'why' notes name what each value encodes and where other data classes have already disagreed). Tweak deliberately: a change here restyles every dataset whose recipe does not pin the knob (approved recipes pin ALL knobs and are immune) and must run the no-regression sweep as a declared delta. starcomb hard-fails if this file and its knob schema drift apart.

why.starless_target: set-03 ladder: 0.12 lit the sky rim (rings 4.4 FAIL); 0.07 is that dataset's safe autostretch target. Class risk: darker-than-necessary sky on clean data.
why.starless_denoise: vstpost is a SELF-FLAT adaptation (noise is radial after V(r) division; every linear placement printed rings there). MEASURED CLASS LIMIT: vstpost crushes object chroma whose contrast sits near the grain scale — M20 wheel -40%, LMC -31..-50%, SMC -47..-50% (A/B probes) — while strong chroma passes untouched (HOO +61 -> -1%, SHO ~nil). On colour-rich moderate-contrast classes ladder this knob FIRST ('off' won on M20, pinned there); 'gx' FAILED the gate on that class (colour 11.0, sky tint). 'gx'/'vst' stay recipe-expressible per class.
why.sep_prom: starsep detection prominence; 4/5/6 sigma all measured ~equal residual on set-03 (faint tail is noise clumping there).
why.sep_engine: auto = StarNet2 when the weights are installed, else inpaint. Engine pins are per-dataset measurements (one exists: a 41-degree MW class where net fails the gate).
why.chroma_core: k=4 from set-03 (colour ~ noise there) and the user's LMC pick. KNOWN CLASS LIMIT: over-neutralizes faint REAL colour — muted LMC Halpha, SMC OIII, M20's blue reflection nebula. The first knob to ladder on colour-rich targets.
why.lum_core: k=2 kills stretch-amplified gray patches; Wiener gate measured structure-safe on a centred galaxy (M74 profile preserved at every radius).
why.core_order: chroma before lum: lum smoothing cannot revive neutralized chroma.
why.stretch_linked: linked is standard on a calibrated stack; unlinked differentially amplifies channel noise (the rainbow engine).
why.satu: 0.2 keeps star colour at -12% fringe cost (fringe span scales ~(1+s)); tuned on set-03 star fringes.
why.cull_pct: 50 was metric-invisible on set-03's star population, and a 0/25/50 ladder on a RICH wheel field (M20) was ALSO visually and metrically null — the thin faint field next to reference finishes lives in the stars MTF anchoring and the starless floor, not the cull.
why.stars_peak: anchor the median top-500 G-basis star amplitude at 0.97 (255 vs 225 at 0.85, set-03 panel pick). The anchor POPULATION is frame/dataset-dependent (top-500 of 22916 vs 852 stars differ in meaning).
why.stars_anchor: catalog = per-dataset star statistics; 'noise' mode exists for same-sky rebuild stability and requires a per-dataset k.
why.stars_floor: 3 sigma skirt coring kills the amplified subtraction-noise aura (+7.0 -> +2.0 on set-03); mechanism argued class-general (it removes only sub-noise signal).
why.black_point: 8/255 output floor from set-03 (bg 16 -> 8; MW gap clipping there was the DESIRED blackness). Class risk: crushes faint extended signal on deeper data.
why.jpg_quality: q100 + 4:4:4: q92/4:2:0 measured mean 2.29 / max 176 counts of star-edge error and hid ~40% of shell chroma. Finals also export lossless PNGs.
why.noise_anchor_k: no generic value EXISTS by construction (k restates one field's star statistics; 11.5x brightness error measured across two real fields); per-dataset recipes only.
